author: brainwashed_lunatic
title: Secretly promoting their own AI slop music and filling their pockets
body:
Never ever listen to a curated playlist by spotify. So many have AI songs that Spotify claims to not create. Even if they don’t create them (which i don’t believe) they still push them to the top. Why? Because the fewer streams real humans get, the less they have to pay. Not to mention this app is unusable without premium. You can’t choose a song that you want. You can’t even listen to a playlist you made without them sneaking their own in. The ads are relentless and I know they’ll only keep jacking up the price because that’s what can squeeze an extra dollar from their listeners into their own grubby pockets. It seems unlikely they’ll let this review stand, but I thought O should get it out there. They are a greedy corporation like any other. Some alternatives are bandcamp, soundcloud, or yt music. Anything but this soulless corporate slop.

Thank you for reading.

author: Miss_Insidious
title: Just meh
body:
If you read most of the “5 star reviews” it’s people complaining here’s my not “5 star review”: 
Used to like using this app, but anymore I hate even looking at it. The “random shuffle” is a lie it plays the same songs over and over constantly and you only get 6 skips after that you don’t have any left so you’re forced to listen to the exact same songs. Just kidding, after every single song there’s 5-6 ads. So you’re not listening to anything at all! Just ads. Constantly. I’ve tracked it if you listen to the 5 ads you’ll get 2 songs before another 5 or 6 ads. 2 whole songs and that’s if you’re lucky! Honestly the audacity the music has to just interrupt this apps advertisements is just awful. That’s all this app even is is just ads the entire time. You reset the app? Sure have 5 ads. You reset again you get 1 song then 6 ads after that. I refuse to give an advertisement app any money to let me listen to a few more songs before getting bombarded with ads ever. Music or podcast app? Nah not even close it’s just an advertisement app with a 2 minute and 15 second song that might play before another 6 ads. Come on dude they’re not even skippable either… if I didn’t like just a handful of songs I’d delete this off my phone now but I’ll make a list of songs soon so I can then.

author: DAVID HUTSON
title: Please read this
body: Look I understand the hype for Spotify. I love it myself, but I don't use it often for reasons I wanna list simply. First one is why do I need premium just to loop a song, rewind a song all that? I feel like that should be for everyone considering all other Music apps allows that to everyone without paying. Second thing is why do y'all randomly shuffle music? Like when I choose a song it plays something completely different. It's stuck on shuffle, because again we have to pay to not feel too "limited" in this app. All my complaints are really on premium, but I got something about the ad's too. Why do y'all randomly bring out over 1 minute ad's?? At max Spotify ad's should be 30 seconds and no higher... I wanna give y'all a chance, but I feel too limited to keep coming back. I hope y'all don't take this in a bad light, but I just wanna be honest on my thoughts and something y'all could actually improve on if y'all listen unlike other companies be doing nowadays. So 3 stars honestly it's still solid for what it is.

author: clownwipes
title: Upping price for slop content
body: This app is simply slop now. The price is ridiculous, even with a student discount it’s hardly worth it. Honestly, ever since Spotify ran ads for ICE and they added their newest and sloppiest AI features I’ve been getting increasingly fed up using this app and being reliant on it for my music listening. It stinks because I’ve used Spotify for almost 10 years now but with the increasing cost, I’m going back to physical media as a way to listen to music. I’m tired of rolling over for the “convenience” it’s only convenient because they’re letting it be and who knows how much longer it’ll be until it’s up there with Netflix living in the land of out-pricing the consumer. 👎👎👎

author: Tiger7stripe
title: Smart shuffle
body: This is terrible it’s been annoying me all day. I just want to listen to a specific artist without the next song being recommended by Spotify. You can only get rid of it with premium I’m not buying premium to pay to listen to music. I can’t even listen to songs without the next song being different than what I want. You can’t change any of it in settings, I searched it up and I did everything it said. Google also said it is a feature forced by Spotify you can only change with the premium. What’s the point of having playlists if it’s just gonna change what you’re listening to. I’ve spent 20 minutes trying to fix it and it’s not working. it’s so frustrating I hate this app so much. My settings don’t even have an option for smart shuffle. If a developer is seeing this please respond to my comment I’m looking for help and I want to keep listening to the music I want.

author: Ethier5751
title: Half and half
body:
So I’ve been a long term user of Spotify since I’ve basically had a phone. I’ve always preferred this app over Apple Music, but recently it’s been glitchy, not loading songs and just all around being slow. I have about 7 thousand liked songs but I will continue to hear the same 10 songs play. I’m getting irritated with this and I’m considering canceling my subscription altogether and switching to Apple Music. It’s a huge disappointment for me because this app was great until recently when it seems to always crash and give me problems. Anyways if you’re going to charge 13 dollars a month I expect better quality out of the app. 
Thanks.

author: j sesh
title: Fix duplicates. Need basic smart playlists. Need mobile lyric page view. Need lyrics cast to TV
body:
2026 Update: There are so many duplicates of songs. So many. Not talking about 10th anniversary versions or single versions but those exist too. I have 4 different duplicates of Maggie Rodger’s Alaska on one of my artist playlists, when they are the exact song (plus I have a single of the same song saved). Each of these are linked to a copy of the standard album. These are not singles, not extended versions, not an anniversary edition, not a greatest hit version, not an acoustic version, just the same song. This clogs my library. Terrible redundancy. Messes up with shuffling into the same song multiple times. I would guess 5-25% of my library would be fixed with this. This also takes more storage on my phone when I want to download my saved songs from an artist. I also dislike when I save a song from the original album then it gets switch to a random compilation of the song and instead of the original artwork and details it shows up as ‘run dance compilation’ with some random artwork. Easily my most frustrating thing with Spotify. 

We need smart playlists. I don’t know why I need to manually add my favorite songs released in 2026 onto a playlist, when I could build that into a playlist then those songs get added automatically when I save them into my library. 

Need a lyric page on mobile. Way frustrating to manually scroll and click to it each time. 

Dream would be to be able to cast lyrics to a TV with karaoke style. So surprising that this is not possible with smart phones and smart TVs. 

Thanks for reading and considering these updates.

author: Nikki J <3
title: Good but not great
body: Been using this music platform for years now. I continue to use it because I think they have a great family plan. But they are very slow on the draw of implementing new features that make the app easy to use. For example, their queue function could use a lot more features like being able to just click a button to move a song to the top instead of having to manually drag it. This app does make a lot of play playlist for you, but if you have a clean version on your personal playlist, they won’t have a clean version of the song on the playlist they make for you—which ruins the playlist for me and so they become useless.

author: Mikej11
title: Love Spotify!
body:
My family and I truly enjoy using Spotify for all of our listening needs. Whether we are creating specialized playlists for workouts, road trips, or my daughter’s passion for theater, Spotify makes it easy to bring the right music and energy into every moment.

We also love being able to listen to powerful, engaging podcasts that entertain, inform, and bring the whole family together. Spotify has become our go-to place for so many different genres, moods, and interests, all in one convenient app.

Thank you, Spotify, for giving our family a place to share music, discover new favorites, and enjoy meaningful listening experiences together. We look forward to continuing to use and enjoy Spotify for years to come.

author: Lagoon Triggerfish
title: Good app at the start
body: For the first few months I had it, it was nice. But recently it’s been playing the same songs from my 100+ song playlist. It’s not much different for the ads either, I’ve only been getting ads for either premium, internet, or the same 2 audiobooks. Whenever it says “30 minutes of uninterrupted listening” I always get two or three ads after not even 15 minutes of songs. I would get premium but honestly the price is ridiculous, I’m not paying $156 a year just to listen to music freely. Which I should be able to because it’s a music app. Spotify had great qualities too, like the easy to navigate app, the amount of songs, and how easy it is to make playlists. But as a non-premium user, I only get 6 skips an hour and I only get to pick my songs so many times a day. I like Spotify but it’s not the same anymore

author: The Ultimate Fan The Second
title: Dismal search, awful information
body:
Search has always been bad - but as the offering increases, you’re often stuck with the first tranche of results with no concept of page two. Adding information to the search usually just adds bad results rather reordering good ones to the top.

There’s no real way see information from a long title. Anything too far left is forever invisible. Tables visible on the desktop app aren’t to be found on the mobile app! Why? Then again in the absence of columns sorting and filtering- tables on the desktop are no good if it doesn’t fit on a single screen.

I can’t define a subsection of a long musical piece in a playlist. In opera that often means you’re stuck with 10 minutes surrounding the 3 minute aria or chorus you want.

These all feel one intern week away from implementing. Boo!


In some places locally searching a playlist now works. Good.

author: jaisifjckfof
body:
Bad.
Don’t bother buying premium. No matter what you’ll do, you’ll have adds. If the app crashes? It’ll show you adds. Did it show you adds before crashing? Doesn’t matter. They’ll take the profit 

It has the potential to be a good app. Sure. But Spotify is no more than a disgusting money grab. It’ll pretend to give you premium (premium means you can actually listen to music) but the second you pay for premium it’ll trap you on the adds only service. Another product ruined by mass media and the need to pay hundreds for music. Sure Spotify may be good, but not as good as other services. Don’t expect my money next month.

author: Alan Grossmith
title: Great app but still missing that key ingredient
body: hence the 4 stars instead of 5.  I have used Spotify for several years and it is great. However, it is still missing one feature that users have asked for time and time again on the Spotify community and that is to allow users to save albums into genre folders. There are workarounds apparently that can be done on a desktop but let’s keep it simple for those on the move; subscribing customers wanting be able to save favourite albums in folders as genres. I would like a go to place where I can pick, for example, an 80’s album from a collection I have centralised in the app. I don’t want to download each individual song into its own single folder as that is not helpful.  It’s in the art of the possible to design, so come on Spotify, give the people what they want and leave the other music apps even further bring. Thank you.

author: BeckyIsMyLife_36
title: Feature creep is slowly ruining this thing
body: Still great for streaming music and giant library but this app was getting so slow on my phone that it discouraged me from using it at all. Everything feels designed in the model of social media now: there’s tiktok-style discovery features and even a messaging feature for some reason. Also every song has four or five different features to scroll down: lyrics, about the artist, this useless “song dna” feature which I’m not sure actually does anything, artist’s mercy, etc. This overload of useless features meant that it would take two minutes at a time for anything to load on my phone, to the point where I had to delete the app altogether and put it onto a dummy phone for my patience’s sake. Maybe this app is just teaching me to appreciate the slow internet of the 90’s, but it needs some genuine redesigning. Please get rid of these nonsensical add-ons, or even make a mode where you can only see the bare minimum possible to use it. Using this app makes me crave old MP3 players with 2cm squared of screen.

author: Emmajane90
title: Very money grabby suddenly
body:
I pay for a family plan and now all the podcasts have come back it’s Constant Ads coming in MUCH louder and far more frequent than someone paying almost £25 a month expects to contend with. Nice relaxing podcast to nod off to, punctuated by the most irritating adverts on skinny jabs (which shouldn’t be promoted in this way imo) and hiring staff I also don’t need. 
Oh and let’s not forget the audiobooks - enjoy the those short reads because once you’re out of your allotted hours, enjoy £20 for a few more! Might give up 8 years of Spotify (£1700 give or take) over to YouTube or something less ad spammy)

author: Psjitaewon
title: App doing too much… performance deteriorating
body: This app has continued to disappoint in the last few months. As they’ve added more and more unwanted content… audio books etc. it’s become slower and slower. Its core purpose is to download and listen to music. It’s glitchy now on ‘downloaded’ playlists on different devices and often can’t access downloaded content offline to listen to. It’s also performing poorly on iPad… have recently replaced to new iPad and still the same problems. Family and friends are also all having problems, not all based in Europe! Get back to your core business Spotify or you will start to lose subscribers to other music services… it’s a shame as this used to be such a good app… but increased costs above inflation year on year, with poorer performance = time to look around for alternatives!
